generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor in the Living Room reporting values in °C."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]
instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor in the Kitchen reporting relative humidity in %."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]
instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "CO₂ sensor in the Conference Room reporting CO₂ concentration in ppm."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor at the Entrance detecting presence in real time."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]
instance PowerConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Power meter in the Hallway measuring consumption in kW."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PowerDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (Data Transmitted)
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]
instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]
instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]
instance PowerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PowerConsumptionSensor
]

// C5: Data (channeling to Enablers)
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current temperature value in °C from TemperatureSensor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]
instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current relative humidity percentage from HumiditySensor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
    DTDFVocab:fromData HumidityDataTransmitted
]
instance CO2Data : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current CO₂ concentration from CO2Sensor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
    DTDFVocab:fromData CO2DataTransmitted
]
instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real‑time occupancy status from OccupancySensor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]
instance PowerConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real‑time power consumption from PowerConsumptionSensor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerDataTransmitted
]

// C11: Enablers
instance HonoMessaging : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono broker providing publish/subscribe for sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]
instance DittoTwinStore : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto twin store persisting device state and enforcing policy validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]
instance UnrealRenderingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 rendering engine consuming twin states for immersive BIM visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]
instance MicroserviceRuntime : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runtime environment for deploying custom analytics and machine‑learning services."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService
]
instance MDEToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model‑Driven Engineering toolkit that auto‑generates deployable twin artifacts from enriched BIM."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractionService
]

// C10: Models (Geometric, Behavioral, Analytical)
instance BuildingGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UE5 3D scene derived from enriched BIM geometry."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealRenderingEngine
]
instance BuildingBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic scripts translating actuator states into behavior within the twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoTwinStore
]
instance BuildingAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Continuous sensor streams and actuator states represented analytically."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMessaging
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real‑time ingestion and monitoring of sensor and actuator data."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureAnomalyInsight,
                HumidityAnomalyInsight,
                CO2AnomalyInsight,
                OccupancyPredictionInsight,
                PowerConsumptionTrendInsight
]
instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates command publication to lighting and HVAC actuators."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACSetPointAction,
                LightPowerStateAction
]
instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization of building geometry with live status overlays."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveBIMOverlayInsight,
                StatusOverlayInsight
]
instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs predictive models for energy usage and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyUsagePredictionInsight,
                OccupancyForecastInsight
]
instance InteractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows DT Engineers to extend twin functionality by publishing new services or analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides AddNewDashboardsAction
]

    base:desc "Detects abnormal temperature fluctuations across the building."
]

    base:desc "Detects abnormal humidity variations in occupied zones."
]

]

]

    base:desc "Command to set the temperature set-point for HVAC units."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    base:desc "Command to toggle light power state and adjust brightness."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    base:desc "Action to add a new reporting dashboard for selected sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting device with power state, brightness, and color‑temperature controls."
]
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC unit controlling temperature set-point for environmental control."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control actions originate from the DT by updating the Ditto twin model which exposes command attributes (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting schedules, actuator states).  These updates are propagated through Hono’s publish‑subscribe channel to the appropriate device endpoints using the same transport protocols as the telemetry path (MQTT, HTTP, etc.).  Commands are wrapped in Ditto’s twin delta messages, optionally signed or authenticated via token or TLS endpoints, and then translated into the actuator’s native command format by a device gateway.  Feedback from the physical device – such as acknowledgment flags or measurement confirmations – is reported back to the twin via the same Hono channel, allowing the DT to resolve conflicts and confirm execution.  The architecture ensures end‑to‑end validation and secure command acceptance, though explicit conflict‑resolution policies are not detailed in the supplied material."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system is designed for continuous, real‑time synchronization between the physical building and its digital counterpart through bidirectional data exchange. While the documents do not specify exact update frequencies or latency thresholds, the architecture supports a real‑time temporal granularity that can accommodate a wide spectrum of services, from raw sensor sampling to higher‑level analytics, without introducing perceptible delays in operational monitoring."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is realized as a modular, service‑oriented framework.  The core framework serves as a central hub that facilitates bidirectional data exchange between the physical building and all connected services.  Integration points are standardized to allow custom services and analytics to be attached without full redeployment, thanks to runtime configuration management.  Key stakeholder roles are represented by distinct services: Building Control Engineers deploy device models into the system; Facility Managers access the running DT for monitoring and control; DT Engineers extend the platform with additional analytics or control logic.  Coordination mechanisms involve a shared data repository and control bus that propagate state changes and command signals across all services, ensuring consistency across the Digital Twin lifecycle stages."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Initial device modelling: Building Control Engineers enrich BIM models with explicit BCS (building control system) components – sensors, actuators, controllers – creating a unified semantic foundation. Model‑Driven Engineering (MDE) extracts structured, machine‑readable representations from the enriched BIM, automatically generating deployable DT artifacts (e.g., RESTful APIs, data models). Deployment phase produces a live DT synchronized in real time with the physical building via continuous data exchange channels. Operations and monitoring are conducted through interactive visualization interfaces that provide immersive, real‑time dashboards. Throughout these phases the framework manages transitions via standardized extension points, enabling runtime evolution (e.g., adding analytics services) while maintaining governance across the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured through a multi‑stage validation pipeline: first, the framework validates enriched BIM models against schema constraints, then transforms them into TwinComponents while preserving semantic mappings. Data validity is enforced by Eclipse Ditto’s policy engine, which checks sensor measurements and actuator commands for unit consistency and admissible value ranges. Fault tolerance is achieved via Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once delivery semantics and Ditto’s persistent event storage, which guarantees message replay in the event of network failures. Additionally, pre‑execution checks on actuator commands prevent unsafe operations, providing operational safety. The approach leverages Model‑Driven Engineering to guarantee traceability between the digital model and the physical assets."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The core connectivity consists of Eclipse Hono, which serves as the device‑to‑cloud messaging broker, and Eclipse Ditto, which provides the twin representation and management layer. Hono receives data from heterogeneous device protocols and forwards them to Ditto’s persistent store, enabling real‑time twin updates. Ditto offers a REST API and an MQTT consumer for twin CRUD operations and real‑time notifications. The architecture follows a centralized publish/subscribe topology, with the broker mediating communication between devices and the twin server. The documents do not specify bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols in detail."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The system is designed to integrate with external infrastructure and enterprise systems through standardized data exchange protocols.  It incorporates BIM tools for importing enriched static models, and sensor networks for injecting real‑time operational data, thereby bridging design and operational data silos.  Interoperability across heterogeneous systems is achieved via the framework’s modular extension points that can interface with third‑party analytics or control modules.  The architecture supports bidirectional communication, allowing external systems to query the DT state and receive updates, while also permitting the DT to push control commands to physical equipment.  No hard‑coded external API formats or specific data standards are mandated, but the framework is intended to accommodate industry‑recognised protocols and formats used in AECO contexts."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adopts BIM as the foundational representation, complying with industry‑accepted BIM standards for building information modeling. It leverages Model‑Driven Engineering (MDE) principles to transform enriched BIM into deployable digital twin artifacts, ensuring semantic alignment with BIM workflows. Extension points are defined in a standardized manner to support plug‑in services, promoting interoperability with external analytics or control systems. While the framework highlights the absence of standardized integration frameworks in the broader AECO domain, it addresses this by providing a coherent, BIM‑centric modeling and deployment pipeline that aligns with existing AECO standards."
]
